title: US and Iran Move Toward Peace with New Preliminary Deal
subtitle: As a formal signing ceremony approaches, leaders face questions from lawmakers about the details of the agreement.

summary.en: The United States and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to end their recent conflict. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday at a remote resort in Switzerland. The deal focuses on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and managing Iran's nuclear program. However, many American lawmakers are expressing concern because they have not yet seen the full details. Further negotiations will take place over the next two months to finalize the terms.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗已達成初步協議以結束近期的衝突。正式簽署儀式定於週五在瑞士的一處偏遠度假村舉行。該協議重點在於重新開放荷莫茲海峽以及管理伊朗的核子計畫。然而，許多美國國會議員因尚未見到完整細節而表示擔憂。未來兩個月將進行進一步談判以敲定條款。

paragraphs.en: The United States and Iran are preparing to sign a preliminary peace deal this Friday at the Burgenstock resort in Switzerland. This agreement aims to end the war between the two nations and restore stability to the region. The Swiss foreign ministry confirmed the location, noting that the site was chosen by mediators from Pakistan and Qatar, as well as by the US and Iran, because it is easy to keep secure.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗正準備於本週五在瑞士的布爾根施托克 (Burgenstock) 度假村簽署一份初步和平協議。這項協議旨在結束兩國之間的戰爭，並恢復該地區的穩定。瑞士外交部證實了該地點，並指出選擇此處是由巴基斯坦與卡達的調解人，以及美國與伊朗共同決定的，因為該地點易於維安。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the agreement is based on a "memorandum of understanding" — a formal document that shows both sides agree on the main goals but have not yet finished all the details. US Vice-President JD Vance described the document as being about a page and a half long. He explained that the most difficult parts of the deal will be decided during a 60-day period of technical negotiations that begins immediately after the signing ceremony.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》(The Guardian) 報導，該協議基於一份「諒解備忘錄」，這是一份正式文件，顯示雙方對主要目標達成共識，但尚未完成所有細節。美國副總統范斯 (JD Vance) 形容該文件長度約為一頁半。他解釋說，協議中最困難的部分將在簽署儀式後立即展開的六十天技術談判期間決定。

paragraphs.en: One of the most important parts of the deal is the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for global oil shipments. The Guardian reports that the US will allow Iran to start selling oil and fuel again immediately, provided that Iran follows the rules of the deal. These rules include keeping the strait open for ships and agreeing not to build a nuclear weapon. Additionally, an Iranian official stated that a two-month US naval blockade on Iranian ports has already been lifted.
paragraphs.zh: 協議中最關鍵的部分之一是重新開放荷莫茲海峽，這是一條對全球石油運輸至關重要的水道。《衛報》報導稱，只要伊朗遵守協議規則，美國將允許伊朗立即恢復石油與燃料銷售。這些規則包括保持海峽對船隻開放，以及同意不製造核子武器。此外，一名伊朗官員表示，美國對伊朗港口實施的兩個月海軍封鎖已經解除。

paragraphs.en: Regarding the nuclear issue, Vice-President Vance confirmed to NBC News that international inspectors from the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) will be allowed to return to Iran. The agency is an international group that checks nuclear sites to ensure they are used only for peaceful purposes. Vance stated that the US and the IAEA will help Iran destroy its supply of highly enriched uranium, which is a material used to create nuclear weapons. There is also discussion about releasing frozen Iranian money and providing financial support to help rebuild the country if Iran meets specific goals.
paragraphs.zh: 關於核子議題，范斯向美國國家廣播公司 (NBC News) 證實，國際原子能總署 (IAEA) 的國際視察員將獲准重返伊朗。該總署是一個國際組織，負責檢查核設施以確保其僅用於和平目的。范斯表示，美國與國際原子能總署將協助伊朗銷毀其高濃縮鈾庫存，這是製造核子武器的原料。此外，若伊朗達成特定目標，雙方也正在討論解凍伊朗資金並提供財政援助，以協助該國重建。

paragraphs.en: Despite the progress, the deal has faced significant questions from members of the US Congress. Many Republican lawmakers have expressed skepticism, which means they are not yet convinced that the deal is a good idea. Senate majority leader John Thune told reporters that he has not been personally briefed on the agreement. He noted that it is difficult to take the deal seriously when the details remain secret. Even some members of the Democratic party, including Senate minority leader Chuck Schumer, have called on the Trump administration to release the full text of the agreement and provide immediate information to Congress.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管有所進展，該協議仍面臨美國國會議員的重大質疑。許多共和黨籍國會議員表達了懷疑態度，意即他們尚未確信這項協議是個好主意。參議院多數黨領袖圖恩 (John Thune) 告訴記者，他尚未親自聽取有關該協議的簡報。他指出，在細節仍保密的情況下，很難認真看待這項協議。甚至連包括參議院少數黨領袖舒默 (Chuck Schumer) 在內的部分民主黨成員，也呼籲川普政府公開協議全文，並立即向國會提供資訊。

paragraphs.en: President Donald Trump has defended the process, stating that he plans to send the deal to Congress for a review. He expressed confidence that the agreement would be approved. Meanwhile, other groups are watching the situation closely. Hezbollah, a group based in Lebanon, has suggested that Iran will push for Israel to pull its troops out of southern Lebanon during the next phase of talks with the United States. However, the group noted that such a withdrawal would be a result of future discussions rather than a requirement for the current deal.
paragraphs.zh: 總統川普為此過程辯護，表示他計畫將協議送交國會審查。他對協議將獲批准表示有信心。與此同時，其他團體正密切關注局勢。總部位於黎巴嫩的真主黨 (Hezbollah) 暗示，伊朗將在與美國的下一階段談判中，推動以色列從黎巴嫩南部撤軍。然而，該組織指出，這種撤軍將是未來討論的結果，而非目前協議的要求。

paragraphs.en: As the Friday ceremony approaches, the world is watching to see how these two nations will move forward. The next two months will be critical, as officials work to turn this short, general document into a complete and lasting peace plan. For now, the focus remains on the formal signing and the hope that the steps taken this week will prevent further conflict in the Middle East.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著週五儀式的臨近，全球都在關注這兩個國家將如何向前邁進。接下來的兩個月至關重要，官員們將致力於將這份簡短、概括性的文件轉化為完整且持久的和平計畫。目前，焦點仍集中在正式簽署儀式上，並期盼本週採取的步驟能防止中東地區發生進一步的衝突。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To end a rule, law, or restriction.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「舉起」，這裡指解除封鎖。文中：Additionally, an Iranian official stated that a two-month US naval blockade on Iranian ports has already been lifted.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To give someone information or instructions about a situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（簡短的），這裡作動詞的被動態。文中：Senate majority leader John Thune told reporters that he has not been personally briefed on the agreement.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To demand or strongly advocate for something to happen.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 極力爭取、推動某事發生。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「推」，這裡指積極爭取某個目標。文中：Hezbollah, a group based in Lebanon, has suggested that Iran will push for Israel to pull its troops out of southern Lebanon during the next phase of talks with the United States.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: US and Iran deal to end war allows Tehran to sell oil and fuel – Middle East crisis live

sources.title: Skeptical Republicans demand details of US-Iran outline peace deal

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Reach Preliminary Deal to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump has announced that a preliminary peace deal with Iran is now signed. The agreement aims to end months of fighting and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz for global oil shipments. While the deal is a major breakthrough, many difficult issues remain to be solved. Both nations have agreed to a 60-day ceasefire to negotiate a more permanent solution. International leaders are watching closely as the agreement faces early tests on the ground.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Sign Preliminary Deal to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump has announced that a preliminary peace deal between the United States and Iran is now signed. The agreement aims to end three months of war and reopen the important Strait of Hormuz for oil shipments. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday in Switzerland. Despite the progress, many difficult issues like Iran's nuclear program remain unresolved. Leaders at the G7 summit are working to ensure the ceasefire holds as both sides continue to negotiate.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Reach Historic Peace Agreement to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have officially reached a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the deal, which mandates an immediate stop to military operations on all fronts. President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement and ordered the end of a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. International leaders from Europe and the Middle East have expressed strong support for the breakthrough. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled to take place in Switzerland on June 19.

quiz.question: What is the primary function of the 60-day period mentioned by Vice-President JD Vance?

options.A: To allow international inspectors to return to Iranian nuclear sites.
options.B: To finalize the complex details of the peace agreement.
options.C: To review the agreement within the US Congress.
options.D: To facilitate the withdrawal of troops from southern Lebanon.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，副總統 JD Vance 指出這份備忘錄僅有一頁半長，而最困難的細節部分將在簽署儀式後的 60 天技術談判期內決定。選項 A 是第四段提到的核查事項；選項 C 是第五段提到的國會審查程序；選項 D 則是第六段提到 Hezbollah 對未來談判的臆測，皆與 60 天談判期的直接目的不符。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might the US Congress be hesitant to support the current peace deal?

options.A: Because the deal requires the immediate destruction of all Iranian infrastructure.
options.B: Because the deal was negotiated primarily by Pakistan and Qatar.
options.C: Because the full details of the agreement have not been disclosed to them.
options.D: Because the deal forces the US to lift a naval blockade before any progress is made.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，參議院多數黨領袖 John Thune 表示他未收到簡報，且細節仍處於保密狀態，這讓國會難以認真看待此協議；少數黨領袖 Chuck Schumer 也要求公開全文。選項 A 與文章內容矛盾；選項 B 提到的是調解國，並非國會猶豫的主因；選項 D 的封鎖已在第三段提到被解除，並非國會擔憂的焦點。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'skepticism' mean?

options.A: A state of doubt or lack of conviction.
options.B: A feeling of intense anger or hostility.
options.C: A sense of confusion regarding complex documents.
options.D: A strong desire to participate in negotiations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到共和黨議員表達 skepticism，並緊接著解釋這意味著他們「尚未確信這項協議是個好主意」。這顯示該詞意指懷疑或不信任。選項 B 的憤怒與選項 C 的困惑在文中並未體現；選項 D 則與懷疑的語意完全相反，議員們並未表現出參與談判的渴望，而是保持距離。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the current state of the US-Iran peace deal?

options.A: The deal is already fully implemented and has received total support from both nations.
options.B: The deal is a finalized treaty that guarantees long-term stability in the Middle East.
options.C: The deal is a preliminary framework that faces significant political scrutiny and future challenges.
options.D: The deal is primarily focused on the removal of troops from Lebanon by the US military.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章開頭提到這是一個「初步（preliminary）」協議，且文中多處提到細節尚待談判（第二段）、國會面臨質疑（第五段）以及未來仍有挑戰（最後一段），這顯示該協議尚未定案且充滿變數。選項 A 與 B 過於樂觀，忽略了文中提到的審查與談判過程；選項 D 僅是 Hezbollah 提到的未來可能議題，並非協議核心。
